meta.meta_description: The latest UK property news, market updates, regulation changes and expert commentary from Advaith Homes.

hero.title: Property News & Insights
hero.description: Stay up to date with the latest UK property market news, mortgage rate changes, regulation updates and expert commentary - all in one place.

featured.label: ⭐ Featured Story
featured.title: UK House Prices Rise for Third Consecutive Month as Demand Outpaces Supply
featured.excerpt: Average property values climbed 1.4% in April, with the North West and East Midlands leading growth, according to the latest ONS House Price Index data.

items.title: Mortgage Rates Fall for Third Month in a Row
items.excerpt: Average two-year fixed rates have dropped to 4.8%, the lowest since early 2023, as lenders compete for market share.

items.title: Renters Reform Bill: What It Means for Landlords and Tenants
items.excerpt: The long-awaited legislation abolishing Section 21 no-fault evictions has passed its final reading in Parliament.

items.title: Stamp Duty Changes: What First-Time Buyers Need to Know in 2024
items.excerpt: The temporary Stamp Duty thresholds introduced in 2022 are set to end - here is how buyers will be affected.

items.title: London vs the Regions: Where is Property Offering the Best Value in 2024?
items.excerpt: New data shows northern cities outperforming the capital on price growth, yield and buyer demand for the second year running.

items.title: Estate Agent Fees Rise Slightly as Competition Intensifies in Spring Market
items.excerpt: Average fees for high street agents have nudged up to 1.42%, while online-only agents are gaining market share in urban areas.

items.title: Bank of England Holds Base Rate at 5.25% - What Next for Borrowers?
items.excerpt: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold, with the two dissenters calling for a cut. Markets now expect the first reduction in August 2024.

items.title: Rightmove Reports Record Spring Asking Prices - Average Home Now £375,131
items.excerpt: New seller asking prices hit an all-time high in May, driven by low supply and improving buyer confidence ahead of a potential rate cut.

items.title: Halifax: Annual House Price Growth Turns Positive for First Time in 14 Months
items.excerpt: The lender's index showed prices up 0.2% year-on-year in April, reversing a sustained period of modest declines.

items.title: New Build Completions Fall 22% Year-on-Year as Construction Costs Bite
items.excerpt: NHBC data shows the sharpest single-year drop in new build registrations since 2009, raising concerns about long-term housing supply.

items.title: Zoopla: Time to Sell Falls to 25 Days in Most Active Regions
items.excerpt: Properties in Yorkshire, the North West and the East Midlands are selling fastest, with competitive pricing the key driver.

items.title: New EPC Requirements for Landlords: What You Must Do by 2025
items.excerpt: All privately rented properties in England and Wales will need at least an EPC rating of C by 2025, affecting an estimated 2.3 million homes.

items.title: Leasehold Reform Act 2024: Key Changes Explained
items.excerpt: The Act makes it easier and cheaper for leaseholders to extend their lease or buy the freehold - here is what has changed and when it takes effect.

items.title: SDLT Surcharge on Second Homes Rises to 5% - What Buyers Need to Know
items.excerpt: The additional Stamp Duty surcharge on additional residential properties rose from 3% to 5% with immediate effect following the Autumn Statement.

items.title: Barclays and Halifax Cut Fixed Rates - What the New Deals Mean for You
items.excerpt: Two of the UK's biggest lenders have cut their headline fixed rates by up to 0.3%, offering a boost to buyers and remortgagers looking to lock in.

items.title: More Borrowers Choosing 5-Year Fixes Over 2-Year Deals - Here's Why
items.excerpt: With rate certainty increasingly valuable, data shows a growing preference for longer-term fixes as borrowers look to shield themselves from future uncertainty.

items.title: Mortgage Approvals Hit Highest Level Since October 2022, Bank of England Data Shows
items.excerpt: Net mortgage approvals for house purchases rose to 61,300 in March, the highest reading in 18 months and a sign of recovering buyer confidence.
